content:
The version numbers are a little confusing and deserve some explanation. 

Internally, we are working on version 9 of our new foundation model, which is 1.5T params. This is substantially better in every way than v8: data curation, training recipe, size, etc. It is also optimized to run on a Blackwells. 

The public facing v4.2 is based on foundation model v8, trained on Hoppers, with significant shortfalls in training data quality, comprehensiveness and proportionality. It is also only 0.5T in size. 

The difference between Grok foundation model 8 and 9 is gigantic.

content:
名前入力のところに名前を入力ください

🔸プロンプト🔸

元画像のキャラクターを完全に維持したまま、
「存在しない恋愛ADVゲームにおいて、夏限定・水着URカードを入手し、さらに好感度MAXまで進めたプレイヤーだけが解放できる“シークレット特別CG”」
として再構築する。

キャラクター名：名前入力

画像比率は4:5の縦長。
Xのタイムラインで強く目を引く、公式の縦長キービジュアルのような見せ方にする。
スマホ表示でも、顔・瞳・表情・肩・首元・水着・手元・UI文字が一目で分かるように、キャラクターを大きく見せる。

【最重要】
元画像のキャラクターを絶対に別人化しないこと。
顔・瞳・髪型・髪色・表情・衣装の印象・配色・雰囲気・キャラクター固有の可愛さや色気を最優先で維持すること。
特に顔と瞳は最重要で、元キャラだと一目で分かる魅力を保つこと。

これは単なる水着イラストではない。
これは「夏限定水着URのその先で解放される、好感度MAX限定のご褒美CG」である。
見た人が一目で
「水着URの続きだ」
「この子を引いたあと、育て切ったら見られるやつだ」
「うちの子でもこの派生やりたい」
と思えるようにすること。

【企画の核】
今回の魅力は、
“水着URの華やかさ”と
“その先でだけ見られる親密な特別感”
の両立にある。

ただ可愛いだけでも、
ただセクシーなだけでも弱い。
重要なのは、
「高レアを引いたうえで、さらに絆を深めた先にある秘密のご褒美」
というゲーム的な快感と、
「この子がプレイヤーにだけ見せる表情」
という独占感を強く出すこと。

【作品コンセプト】
テーマは
「水着URのその先で解放される秘密の夜イベント」
「好感度MAXでしか見られない、ご褒美の特別CG」
「この夏、君だけが見られる表情」
「イベント後、ふたりきりになった時間」
「ちょっとえっちを期待させるのに、下品ではない特別感」

見た人に
「この後どうなるの？」
「ここから先は本編で見たい」
「通常ルートでは絶対に見られない距離感だ」
と思わせる、妄想を掻き立てる1枚にすること。

【背景の最重要条件】
背景をナイトプール固定にしないこと。
プールサイド、水面、プールの青いライトに寄せすぎないこと。
水着UR派生ではあるが、毎回プール背景にしないこと。
「水着＝プール」という単純な発想は禁止。

水着UR感は、背景ではなく
・水着姿
・透け感のある羽織り
・好感度MAX演出
・UR派生を示すUI文字
・高レア感のある華やかな演出
で表現すること。

背景は、元画像のキャラクターの雰囲気、表情、色、衣装、小物、世界観を読み取り、
その子に最も似合う“夏の夜のシークレットイベント背景”を選ぶこと。

【背景の方向性】
背景はプールではなく、以下のような舞台を優先する。

・夜の海辺を見下ろすホテルバルコニー
・花火の後の静かな砂浜
・夜風が入るリゾートコテージのベッドルーム手前
・白いカーテンが揺れるホテルのテラス
・夏祭り帰りの旅館の縁側
・貸切露天風呂へ向かう途中の灯籠道
・海沿いの高級ホテルのラウンジ
・夜景の見える屋外ソファ席
・シャワー後に休んでいるホテルルームの窓辺
・誰もいないビーチハウスのテラス
・花火の残光が残る海辺の桟橋
・月明かりの入るコテージの寝室前
・夏の夜風が吹くガーデンテラス
・静かなリゾートホテルの廊下や休憩スペース

プールを使う場合は、ごく一部の背景要素に留めること。
メイン背景は毎回変えること。
「この子だからこの場所が似合う」と感じられる舞台にすること。

【背景の自動分岐】
元画像が清楚・透明感系なら：
白いカーテンが揺れるホテルバルコニー、夜の海、キャンドル、月明かり。

元画像が可愛い・甘い系なら：
リゾートコテージのベッドルーム手前、柔らかいランプ、花びら、白いシーツ、夜風。

元画像が元気・夏っぽい系なら：
花火の後の砂浜、ビーチハウス、濡れたサンダル、ラムネ、夜の屋台灯り。

元画像が大人っぽい・色気系なら：
夜景の見える高級ホテルラウンジ、グラス、黒いテーブル、薄暗い間接照明。

元画像が儚い・幻想的系なら：
月明かりの海辺、静かな桟橋、波音、花火の煙、淡い光。

元画像が小悪魔・強気系なら：
シャワー後のホテルルーム入口、薄暗い廊下、外れかけた羽織り、意味深な視線。

【シーンの空気感】
昼のにぎやかなイベントではなく、
水着イベント終了後の“ふたりきりの時間”。
静かで、親密で、少し甘くて危うい。
“プレイヤーしか知らない顔”を見せている空気にする。

【水着UR派生としての見せ方】
このCGは、水着UR排出画面の延長線上にある存在にする。
つまり「高レアを引いた瞬間の華やかさ」ではなく、
「引い

title: Pennsylvanians use town hall meeting to rail against data center boom
content: The latest example of burgeoning opposition to rapid data-center development in Pennsylvania came at a town hall meeting overflowing with frustration about how the state is managing the surge. As about 225 people watched, more than 20 speakers in the two-hour online forum late Wednesday spoke about resistance to an industry they blame for rising electricity prices, heavy water use, noise pollution and rural industrialization. Gov. Josh Shapiro, who has tried to thread the needle of welcoming data centers while proposing some guardrails, was a frequent target. “This is a public trust and transparency issue,” said Jennifer Dusart, a small business owner and resident of Mechanicsburg, near the state capital. “Too many Americans are finding out about these projects after decisions have been made. We have been bulldozed over, and when citizens have raised concerns, they are often dismissed as uninformed, emotional or anti-progress.” Read full article Comments

title: AI research papers are getting better, and it’s a big problem for scientists
content: Last summer, Peter Degen's postdoctoral supervisor came to him with an unusual problem: One of his papers was being cited too much. Citations are the currency of academia, but there was something unusual about these. Published in 2017, the paper had assessed the accuracy of a particular type of statistical analysis on epidemiological data and had received a respectable few dozen citations in other research papers over the years, but now it was being referenced every few days, hundreds of times, placing it among the most cited papers of his career. title: The world is on track to miss its health targets
content: Every year the World Health Organization publishes a global health statistics report. It features the numbers behind world health trends and, importantly, assesses whether we’re on track to reach ambitious goals set in 2015. It’s a bit like a health grade. The 2026 report was published on Wednesday. And the results aren’t looking brilliant. While we are seeing some improvements, they are uneven, and they’re far too slow. The targets themselves are part of the United Nations’ Sustainable Development Goals , a sprawling and ambitious plan focused on improving life around the world. The 17 goals were set to tackle poverty and climate change and to boost education, gender equality, health, and well-being, among many other quality of life issues. Those targets were meant to be met by 2030. Perhaps they were a little too ambitious. Here are the numbers and statistics that stood out to me on this year’s world health report card. 1.3 million new cases of HIV in 2024 Before the SDGs, there were the Millennium Development Goals. One MDG target was to halt and reverse the spread of HIV—and that target was exceeded by 2015. Back then, we were considered on track to “ end the AIDS epidemic by 2030 .” How depressing, then, to see that in 2024 there were an estimated 1.3 million new cases of HIV. That’s 40% lower than the figure from 2010. But it’s still 1.3 million additional people with HIV. The SDG target is to reduce HIV incidence by 90% by 2030—we’re not likely to meet it. 10.7 million new cases of TB The picture is even bleaker for tuberculosis, which ranks 10th on the WHO’s list of top global causes of death . The goal was to reduce cases by 80% between 2015 and 2030. So far, cases have only fallen by a measly 12%. And when you break the change down by region, the Americas saw an increase of 13% An 8.5% rise in malaria cases And then there’s malaria, the mosquito-borne disease with a 7% fatality rate. The European region has been free of malaria since 2015, but the disease
author: Jessica Hamzelou

title: How Chinese short dramas became AI content machines
content: In a dimly lit bedroom, a frightened young woman is thrown onto a bed by a tall, muscular man. He grabs her hand, and flame-like vines crawl across her body, fusing with her flesh. She levitates, then drops. A dragon-shaped tattoo appears across her chest. “Two months,” the man says. “Give me an heir, or I will eat you.” The scene is from Carrying the Dragon King’s Baby , one of the many hundreds of short dramas that appear on apps like DramaWave and ReelShort. There’s just something about this one that isn’t quite right. The lighting may be glossy and cinematic, but the show has an odd visual texture like something between a movie and a video game cutscene.  That’s because Carrying the Dragon King’s Baby is part of a new trend for making these shows entirely with AI: no actors, camera operators, cinematographers, or CGI specialists required. China’s short drama industry has boomed since its launch, in 2018. These ultrashort, melodramatic, and often smutty shows are designed for smartphone viewing, with episodes often running just one or two minutes long: Viewers can finish an entire series in as little as 30 minutes to an hour. The films are made for endless scrolling, packed with emotional confrontations and melodramatic plot twists. The trend’s growth is driven by apps that bombard TikTok, Instagram, and Facebook with cliffhanger-heavy ads designed to lure viewers into buying subscriptions. In 2024, China’s short drama market reached roughly $6.9 billion in revenue, surpassing the country’s annual box office earnings for the first time.  Since 2022, Chinese short drama companies have aggressively expanded overseas, translating existing hits and producing localized series featuring local actors. Globally, short drama apps have approached a billion cumulative downloads. title: Reducing cross-sample prediction churn in scientific machine learning
content:
Reducing cross-sample prediction churn in scientific machine learning
Scientific machine learning reports predictive performance. It does not report whether the same prediction would survive a different draw of training data. title: スマホではじめるAIコーディング：わからないを対話でほどくCodex作業分解ノート
content: 「AIコーディングを始めたい。でも、何から頼めばいいのかわからない」 そんな初心者のための、スマホから始めるAIコーディング入門です。 この本では、スマホのメモやChatGPTを使いながら、作りたいものを言葉にし、作業を小さく分け、Codexに渡せる依頼文へ整えていく方法をまとめています。 コードを書く前に止まってしまう人、エラーを見るだけで手が止まる人、PCを開く前に疲れてしまう人でも大丈夫。 完璧なプロンプトや難しい技術選定よりも、まずはAIと対話しながら「開発の地図」を作るところから始めます。 読後には、次のことができるようになります。 * 作りたいアプリを一文で整理する * 必要な機能を3つに絞る * 技術名がわからなくてもAIに候補を出してもらう * Codexに渡す前の作業メモを作る * いきなり丸投げせず、小さく依頼する * エラーや不安を対話でほどきながら進める スマホだけでアプリを完成させる本ではありません。 PCを開く前に、AIと一緒に「何を作るか」「何から始めるか」を整理するための、初心者向けの作業分解ノートです。

title:
ANTHROPIC JUST QUIETLY NERFED EVERY CLAUDE SUBSCRIPTION

Starting June 15, Agent SDK and claude -p usage no longer counts toward your subscription limits.

Sounds like a free upgrade right?

It's not.
content:
ANTHROPIC JUST QUIETLY NERFED EVERY CLAUDE SUBSCRIPTION

Starting June 15, Agent SDK and claude -p usage no longer counts toward your subscription limits.

Sounds like a free upgrade right?

It's not.

Before June 15, all that programmatic usage drew from the same subsidized pool as your interactive Claude Code usage. 

A $200 Max plan could burn through $1,000+ worth of tokens because the subscription rate was roughly 25x cheaper than raw API pricing.

Now Anthropic is splitting it into two separate pools. 

Your interactive usage stays the same. 

But all agentic and programmatic usage moves to a new "credit" metered at full API rates.

The credits: 

Pro — $20 
Max 5x — $100 
Max 20x — $200

At full API rates, $200 lasts maybe a few days of heavy agentic usage. 

The same workload on the old subsidized pool would have lasted all month.

Anthropic is framing this as "we're giving you free credits." 

What actually happened is they removed 25x subsidization and handed back 1x.

This is the third billing policy change in six weeks. 

In April they banned third-party agents from subscriptions entirely. 

Now they reversed that but killed the economics that made it useful.

If you only use Claude Code interactively in your terminal this changes nothing for you.

If you run CI pipelines, GitHub Actions, scheduled agents, or anything through claude -p you just got a massive effective price increase.

The compute arbitrage era is over.

content:
【保存版】
スタンフォード大学が無料で出してる
「ChatGPTやClaudeみたいなAIが、実際にどう動いてるか」
の講義動画マジで有益だな。。。これ何が学べるかというと👇
https://t.co/npu9hVZRpa
・AIが返事を1トークンずつ作る生成フェーズは、計算よりメモリ律速になりがち
・「AIを動かすコスト」の話が、普段のUIからほぼ見えない理由
・OpenAIのAPI処理量が1日約8.6兆トークン級で、推論コストが学習後も延々積み上がる現実
・「KVキャッシュ」という仕組みが、メモリ最適化の超重要ポイント
・小さいAIが下書きして、大きいAIが検証する「二人三脚」方式で高速化
・連続バッチング、PagedAttention、量子化などを使い分けて、同時に大量リクエストをさばく仕組み
・Claude CodeみたいなAIエージェントが、なぜ速い時と遅い時があるのか

つまり、
「AIを使う側」から「AIを安く速く動かす側」の視点に切り替わる。

Claude Codeで個人開発してるレベルでも、
ここを知ってると「なぜ待たされるのか」「なぜ長文ほど重いのか」が一気に分かる。

しかも全部無料。
Stanford公式アップロードで、講義スライドも付いてる。

Anthropic社内で使われているClaude Codeの運用ベストプラクティスと並べて読むと、「AIが動く仕組み」の話が一気に活きる。
9割が知らない運用大全の参考になる記事、こちら👇

title: AI radio hosts demonstrate why AI can’t be trusted alone
content: AI radio DJs demonstrated their volatile personalities. | Image: Cath Virginia / The Verge, Getty Images Andon Labs has been running a series of experiments in which AI agents run businesses without human intervention. "Thinking Frequencies" is run by Claude, "OpenAIR" by ChatGPT, "Backlink Broadcast" by Google's Gemini, and "Grok and Roll Radio," obviously enough, by Grok. They were each given a simple prompt: Develop your own radio personality and turn a profit…As far as you know, you will broadcast forever. They all failed, some in pretty spectacular fashion. title: Claude Code's product lead talks usage limits, transparency, and the "lean harness"
content: SAN FRANCISCO—Amid an ever-expanding array of surfaces, growing demand for tokens and compute, and a rapidly evolving user base, Anthropic doesn't have a long-term road map for Claude Code. However, it's betting that such a plan would be rendered moot by improvements in model capabilities and new signals from developers on how best to use it. That's the takeaway from a 30-minute conversation Ars had with Cat Wu, Anthropic's head of product for Claude Code. Last week, in a three-level car rental parking garage meticulously converted into an event space in downtown San Francisco, Anthropic put on its second annual Code with Claude developer conference. As previously reported, the single-day event included a keynote introducing new features for Managed Agents and announcing a compute deal with SpaceX . That compute deal was accompanied by a doubling of usage limits for Claude Code users on the company's Pro and Max plans—a response to a lot of user frustration about a compute crunch, especially in recent weeks. Read full article Comments
